game_name: Heroes of Might and Magic III

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players control single heroes commanding mythic creature armies
Contesting resource posts for steady income to develop towns
Using collected resources to develop and upgrade towns
Unit movement, positioning, and attack resolution on grid
Strategic spell selection to influence battle outcomes
Collecting and equipping artifacts for stat bonuses
Diplomacy and other negotiation skills to avoid combat
Underground navigation requiring dungeon faction understanding
Finding legendary artifact to build permanent structure with major bonuses
Navigating eight distinct town factions with different playstyles

typical_tension_triggers: Entering tactical combat on hexagonal grid
Holy Grail search in contested territory
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based pacing allows player-controlled tension management; campaign structure alternates between exploration/development phases and high-stakes battles; seven campaigns provide escalating difficulty through shifting perspective (alternating faction viewpoints)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Subterranean layer provides secondary exploration space; larger maps encourage off-path resource hunting

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction to hero command, resource gathering, and basic town development in safe contexts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of hexagonal tactical combat, artifact collection, subterranean exploration, and multi-hero management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Holy Grail hunting, eight-faction political complexity, permanent structure building with major strategic bonuses, campaign objective completion under pressure
notable_spikes: First major tactical battle transition from exploration
Subterranean layer first entry requiring dungeon faction knowledge
Holy Grail objective campaigns with escalated enemy pressure
Final campaign chapters with coordinated multi-faction threats

notable_level_patterns.description: Seven playable campaigns across continent of Antagarich, each telling story from alternating faction perspective; creates escalating narrative complexity and forced perspective shifts
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Erathia campaign - Queen Catherine's restoration arc

notable_level_patterns.description: Cycle of exploring map, contesting resource posts, gathering loot, upgrading towns, then pushing into contested territory; turn-based pacing allows deliberate escalation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early campaign scenarios with accessible mines and neutral posts

notable_level_patterns.description: Different victory conditions including Holy Grail artifact location, timed challenges, and standard town capture; creates distinct pacing within campaign structure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Holy Grail specific maps requiring extended exploration

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign narrative cycles through eight factions (nine with expansion), forcing player adaptation to different unit types, abilities, and strategic approaches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaigns alternating between Erathia (Royal), Nighon (Dungeon), Eeofol (Infernal), Tatalia, Krewlod perspectives

notable_level_patterns.description: Underground maps expand exploration space and introduce vertical axis strategy; optional but strategically valuable for advanced players
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late campaign scenarios with dungeon access points
